zh_zai_location.title: 在 for location
zh_zai_location.shortExplanation: 在 is used to say where someone or something is.
zh_zai_location.fullExplanation: Use 在 before a place to show location. It can also introduce the place where an action happens.

zh_le_completed_action.title: 了 for completed past action
zh_le_completed_action.shortExplanation: Verb + 了 often shows that an action was completed.
zh_le_completed_action.fullExplanation: When 了 follows a verb, it usually marks a completed event. It often refers to something that happened and finished.

zh_question_ne.title: 呢 for follow-up questions
zh_question_ne.shortExplanation: 呢 is often used to ask back about the same topic.
zh_question_ne.fullExplanation: After one person gives information, 呢 can ask 'and you?' or 'what about...?' It often follows a noun or pronoun.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì lǎoshī, nǐ māma ne?

zh_basic_svo.title: Basic SVO word order
zh_basic_svo.shortExplanation: Chinese basic sentences usually follow Subject + Verb + Object order.
zh_basic_svo.fullExplanation: In simple Chinese statements, the normal order is Subject + Verb + Object. Time, place, and manner often come before the main verb. This basic order helps learners build clear sentences early.

zh_time_before_verb.title: Time expressions before the verb
zh_time_before_verb.shortExplanation: Time words usually come before the main verb.
zh_time_before_verb.fullExplanation: In Chinese, time expressions are usually placed after the subject and before the main verb. This helps set the time frame before describing the action. This position is very common in everyday speech.

zh_shi_noun_equation.title: 是 for identity and classification
zh_shi_noun_equation.shortExplanation: 是 connects a subject with a noun or identity.
zh_shi_noun_equation.fullExplanation: Use 是 when saying who someone is, what something is, or what category something belongs to. It is commonly used with jobs, identities, and classifications.

zh_shi_existence.title: 是 indicating existence or presence in a place or time
zh_shi_existence.shortExplanation: 是 can introduce what is present in a certain place, time, or situation.
zh_shi_existence.fullExplanation: In some simple identifying sentences, Chinese uses 是 to say what is there in a place, time, or visible situation. This pattern presents what something is, rather than using 有 to introduce an indefinite item.

examples.pinyin: Zhuō shàng shì nǐ yào de cáiliào.

zh_adjective_predicate_no_shi.title: Adjectives as predicates without 是
zh_adjective_predicate_no_shi.shortExplanation: Chinese adjectives can directly describe the subject without 是.
zh_adjective_predicate_no_shi.fullExplanation: When an adjective is the main predicate, Chinese usually does not use 是. A degree word such as 很 is often added in neutral statements.

zh_question_ma.title: 吗 yes-no questions
zh_question_ma.shortExplanation: Add 吗 at the end of a statement to make a yes-no question.
zh_question_ma.fullExplanation: The simplest way to ask a yes-no question in Chinese is to keep the statement order and add 吗 at the end.

zh_question_wh_words.title: Question words
zh_question_wh_words.shortExplanation: Chinese question words stay in the normal sentence position.
zh_question_wh_words.fullExplanation: Words like 什么, 谁, 哪儿, 怎么, 几, and 什么时候 replace the unknown part of the sentence. They usually stay where the answer would go.

zh_question_a_not_a.title: A-not-A questions
zh_question_a_not_a.shortExplanation: Repeat the verb or adjective in positive and negative form to ask a yes-no question.
zh_question_a_not_a.fullExplanation: Chinese often forms yes-no questions with patterns like A 不 A and 是不是. This is common in daily speech.

examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā diàn guì bú guì?

zh_negation_bu.title: 不 for general negation
zh_negation_bu.shortExplanation: 不 is used to negate verbs, adjectives, and habitual actions.
zh_negation_bu.fullExplanation: Use 不 before a verb or adjective to say 'not' in present, future, or general situations.

zh_negation_mei_you.title: 没 / 没有 for past and possession negation
zh_negation_mei_you.shortExplanation: 没(有) is used for 'did not' and 'do not have'.
zh_negation_mei_you.fullExplanation: Use 没 or 没有 to say something did not happen or that someone does not have something. 没有 is also the normal negative form of 有.

zh_you_possession.title: 有 indicating possession
zh_you_possession.fullExplanation: Use 有 to say that someone has something. Its negative form is 没有, not 不有.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yì běn Zhōngwén shū.

zh_you_existence.title: 有 indicating existence
zh_you_existence.shortExplanation: 有 can mean 'there is' or 'there are'.
zh_you_existence.fullExplanation: Use 有 to introduce the existence of something in a place, time, or situation. This is especially common when the thing is new or indefinite.

examples.pinyin: Zhuōzi shàng yǒu yì běn shū.

zh_you_indefinite_subject.title: 有 introducing an indefinite subject
zh_you_indefinite_subject.shortExplanation: 有 can introduce a new, indefinite person or thing.
zh_you_indefinite_subject.fullExplanation: Chinese often uses 有 when introducing a person or thing that is not yet known in the conversation. This is common with expressions of place or time.

examples.pinyin: Wàimiàn yǒu yí ge rén zài děng nǐ.

examples.chinese: 昨天有很多人来参加活动。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān yǒu hěn duō rén lái cānjiā huódòng.

examples.chinese: 楼下有一家新开的面馆。
examples.pinyin: Lóuxià yǒu yì jiā xīn kāi de miànguǎn.

zh_you_past_action_question.title: 有 in affirmative-negative questions about past action
zh_you_past_action_question.shortExplanation: 有没有 can ask whether an action happened or not.
zh_you_past_action_question.fullExplanation: In spoken Chinese, 有没有 is often used to ask whether something happened, especially in past or completed situations. It is very common in conversation.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuótiān yǒu méiyǒu qù shàngkè?
Tā yǒu méiyǒu gěi nǐ dǎ diànhuà?
Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu kànjiàn wǒ de bāo?

zh_modal_can_permission.title: Permission with 可以
zh_modal_can_permission.shortExplanation: 可以 is used to ask for or give permission.
zh_modal_can_permission.fullExplanation: Use 可以 to say that something is allowed. It is very common in requests and everyday situations.

zh_modal_neng_possibility.title: Possibility with 能
zh_modal_neng_possibility.shortExplanation: 能 can mean that something is possible because conditions allow it.
zh_modal_neng_possibility.fullExplanation: Use 能 when a person is able to do something because circumstances make it possible.

zh_modal_hui_ability.title: Ability or skill with 会
zh_modal_hui_ability.shortExplanation: 会 is used for learned ability or skill.
zh_modal_hui_ability.fullExplanation: Use 会 to say that someone knows how to do something because they learned it.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ huì zuò Zhōngguó cài ma?

zh_modal_xiang_wishing.title: Wishing with 想
zh_modal_xiang_wishing.shortExplanation: 想 is used to express wishes and desires.
zh_modal_xiang_wishing.fullExplanation: Use 想 before a verb to say what someone wants to do. It sounds softer than 要.

zh_modal_yao_future_will.title: Future meaning with 要
zh_modal_yao_future_will.shortExplanation: 要 can express plan, intention, or near future.
zh_modal_yao_future_will.fullExplanation: Use 要 before a verb to express a plan, intention, or something that is going to happen soon.

examples.pinyin: Tā xià ge yuè yào bānjiā.

zh_le_change_of_state.title: 了 for change or reversal of a previous situation
zh_le_change_of_state.shortExplanation: Sentence-final 了 often shows that a new situation has appeared.
zh_le_change_of_state.fullExplanation: When 了 appears at the end of a sentence, it often means that a state has changed. The focus is on the new situation, not only on past time.

zh_measure_words_basic.title: Basic measure words
zh_measure_words_basic.shortExplanation: A number usually needs a measure word before a noun.
zh_measure_words_basic.fullExplanation: In Chinese, numbers normally do not go directly before nouns. Use Number + Measure Word + Noun.

zh_de_possession.title: 的 for possession and simple modification
zh_de_possession.shortExplanation: 的 links an owner or modifier to a noun.
zh_de_possession.fullExplanation: Use 的 to connect an owner, description, or other modifier to a noun. It is one of the most common particles in Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì xīn lái de lǎoshī.
Nàge shì wǒmen gōngsī de chē.

zh_because_because_of_therefore.title: Giving reasons: 因为, 由于, 所以
zh_because_because_of_therefore.shortExplanation: These forms express reasons and results.
zh_because_because_of_therefore.fullExplanation: 因为 and 由于 introduce reasons, while 所以 introduces the result. 因为 is more common in everyday speech, and 由于 is slightly more formal.

examples.chinese: 因为今天下雨，所以我没出去。
examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi jīntiān xiàyǔ, suǒyǐ wǒ méi chūqù.

examples.chinese: 由于路上很堵，我们迟到了。
examples.pinyin: Yóuyú lùshang hěn dǔ, wǒmen chídào le.

examples.chinese: 他因为身体不舒服，所以请假了。
examples.pinyin: Tā yīnwèi shēntǐ bù shūfu, suǒyǐ qǐngjià le.

zh_since_inference_jiran.title: Making inferences with 既然
zh_since_inference_jiran.shortExplanation: 既然 means 'since' when the speaker treats something as already known.
zh_since_inference_jiran.fullExplanation: Use 既然 when the reason is already accepted or obvious, and then give a conclusion or decision. It is often followed by 就.

examples.chinese: 既然你没时间，我们就下次再约吧。
examples.pinyin: Jìrán nǐ méi shíjiān, wǒmen jiù xià cì zài yuē ba.

examples.chinese: 既然来了，就进去看看吧。
examples.pinyin: Jìrán lái le, jiù jìnqù kànkan ba.

examples.chinese: 既然大家都同意，我就不多说了。
examples.pinyin: Jìrán dàjiā dōu tóngyì, wǒ jiù bù duō shuō le.

zh_only_if_only_when.title: Stating conditions with 只有...才...
zh_only_if_only_when.shortExplanation: 只有...才... means 'only if' or 'only when'.
zh_only_if_only_when.fullExplanation: Use 只有 before the condition and 才 before the result to show that the result happens only in that condition.

examples.chinese: 只有多练习，你才会进步。
examples.pinyin: Zhǐyǒu duō liànxí, nǐ cái huì jìnbù.

examples.chinese: 只有下班以后，我才有时间。
examples.pinyin: Zhǐyǒu xiàbān yǐhòu, wǒ cái yǒu shíjiān.

examples.chinese: 只有你自己想学，别人才能帮你。
examples.pinyin: Zhǐyǒu nǐ zìjǐ xiǎng xué, biérén cái néng bāng nǐ.

zh_concession_suiran_danshi.title: Offering concessions: 虽然...但是...
zh_concession_suiran_danshi.fullExplanation: Use 虽然 before one fact and 但是 before another fact that contrasts with it.

examples.chinese: 虽然很晚，但是他还在工作。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán hěn wǎn, dànshì tā hái zài gōngzuò.

examples.chinese: 虽然这个菜有点辣，但是很好吃。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán zhège cài yǒudiǎn là, dànshì hěn hǎochī.

examples.chinese: 虽然我很累，但是我还想去。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán wǒ hěn lèi, dànshì wǒ hái xiǎng qù.

zh_time_when_after_before_as_soon_as.title: Clarifying time: when, after, before, as soon as
zh_time_when_after_before_as_soon_as.shortExplanation: Chinese uses common patterns to organize actions in time.
zh_time_when_after_before_as_soon_as.fullExplanation: 的时候 means 'when', 以后 means 'after', 以前 or 之前 means 'before', and 一...就... means 'as soon as'. These forms are essential for daily narration.

examples.chinese: 我到家的时候已经很晚了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ dào jiā de shíhou yǐjīng hěn wǎn le.

examples.chinese: 吃完饭以后，我们去散步了。
examples.pinyin: Chī wán fàn yǐhòu, wǒmen qù sànbù le.

examples.chinese: 我一下课就给你打电话。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yí xiàkè jiù gěi nǐ dǎ diànhuà.

zh_passive_bei_basic.title: Passive voice with 被
zh_passive_bei_basic.shortExplanation: 被 is used when the focus is on the receiver of an action.
zh_passive_bei_basic.fullExplanation: Use 被 when you want to focus on the person or thing affected by an action. The doer may be expressed or omitted.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ de chē bèi biérén zhuàng le.
Zhè běn shū bèi tā jiè zǒu le.
Mén bèi fēng chuī kāi le.

zh_ba_disposal_basic.title: Introducing an object with 把
zh_ba_disposal_basic.shortExplanation: 把 brings the object before the verb and focuses on what happens to it.
zh_ba_disposal_basic.fullExplanation: Use 把 when the speaker wants to highlight how an object is handled, changed, moved, or finished. The verb phrase usually includes a result, direction, or clear outcome.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bǎ zuòyè xiě wán le.

examples.chinese: 他把手机放在桌子上了。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ shǒujī fàng zài zhuōzi shàng le.

zh_de_degree_all_functions.title: 得 all core functions
zh_de_degree_all_functions.shortExplanation: 得 is used after verbs to introduce complements of degree, result, or possibility, and before verbs to express necessity.
zh_de_degree_all_functions.fullExplanation: After a verb, 得 introduces information about how well, how much, or how successfully an action is done. Before a verb, 得 can mean 'must' or 'have to'. These are different grammar patterns and should be learned separately.

zh_di_adverbial.title: 地 for adverbial modification
zh_di_adverbial.shortExplanation: 地 links a descriptive phrase to a verb.
zh_di_adverbial.fullExplanation: Use 地 when a word or phrase describes how an action is done. It often corresponds to 'in a ... way' or '-ly' in English.

zh_zhe_durative_state.title: 着 for continuing state
zh_zhe_durative_state.shortExplanation: 着 often shows a continuing state or position.
zh_zhe_durative_state.fullExplanation: Use 着 after some verbs to describe a continuing state, such as something staying open, hanging, standing, or being worn.

examples.pinyin: Tā chuān zhe yí jiàn hēi wàitào.
Zhuōzi shàng fàng zhe hěn duō shū.

zh_zhe_doing_sth.title: 着 doing one thing while another happens
zh_zhe_doing_sth.shortExplanation: 着 can connect one ongoing action or state with another action.
zh_zhe_doing_sth.fullExplanation: In patterns like V着V, the first action or state continues while the second action happens. This is common in narration and description.

zh_le_duration.title: 了 describing duration of an action or state
zh_le_duration.shortExplanation: Time expressions with 了 can show how long something has lasted.
zh_le_duration.fullExplanation: Use a duration expression with 了 to show that an action or state started earlier and has lasted until now, or to show how long a completed event lasted depending on context.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xué Zhōngwén xué le liǎng nián le.

examples.chinese: 他在北京住了五个月了。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài Běijīng zhù le wǔ ge yuè le.

zh_result_dao.title: Result complement 到
zh_result_dao.shortExplanation: 到 often shows that a target or result is reached.
zh_result_dao.fullExplanation: When 到 follows a verb, it often means the action successfully reaches something, such as hearing, finding, seeing, receiving, or arriving at a result.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhǎo dào yàoshi le ma?

zh_result_dong.title: Result complement 懂
zh_result_dong.fullExplanation: When 懂 follows a verb such as 听, 看, or 说, it means that the speaker understands successfully.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ kàn dǒng zhège yìsi le ma?
Tā shuō de tài kuài, wǒ méi tīng dǒng.

zh_result_wan.title: Result complement 完
zh_result_wan.shortExplanation: 完 shows that an action is finished completely.
zh_result_wan.fullExplanation: Use 完 after a verb to show that something has been done to the end.

examples.pinyin: Tā gāng kàn wán nà běn xiǎoshuō.

zh_result_zhu.title: Result complement 住
zh_result_zhu.shortExplanation: 住 often shows firmness, stopping, or successful control.
zh_result_zhu.fullExplanation: After another verb, 住 can mean holding firmly, stopping movement, or keeping something under control. It is common in spoken Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Tā yì bǎ zhuā zhù le wǒ de shǒu.

zh_directional_movement_basic.title: Directional complements: 来, 去, 过, 上, 下
zh_directional_movement_basic.shortExplanation: These complements show the direction of movement.
zh_directional_movement_basic.fullExplanation: 来 means movement toward the speaker or reference point, 去 means movement away, 过 often means crossing over, 上 indicates upward movement, and 下 indicates downward movement. They combine with verbs to show detailed direction.

examples.pinyin: Háizi pǎo shàng lóu qù le.

zh_modal_probability_hui.title: Probability with 会
zh_modal_probability_hui.shortExplanation: 会 can also express what is likely to happen.
zh_modal_probability_hui.fullExplanation: Besides learned ability, 会 can mean future probability or expectation. In this use, it often means 'will probably' or 'is likely to'.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhèyàng zuò huì gèng kuài.

zh_modal_obligation_should.title: Obligation with 应该 and 得
zh_modal_obligation_should.shortExplanation: 应该 and 得 can express obligation or necessity.
zh_modal_obligation_should.fullExplanation: 应该 is often used for advice or expectation, while 得 before a verb can mean that something is necessary or required.

zh_modal_willingness_yuanyi.title: Willingness with 愿意
zh_modal_willingness_yuanyi.shortExplanation: 愿意 is used to express willingness.
zh_modal_willingness_yuanyi.fullExplanation: Use 愿意 to say that someone is willing to do something. It often carries a personal or emotional choice.

examples.pinyin: Tā bù yuànyì shuō zhè jiàn shì.

examples.chinese: 你愿意跟我们一起去吗？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ yuànyì gēn wǒmen yìqǐ qù ma?

zh_you_degree_extent.title: 有 specifying degree or extent
zh_you_degree_extent.shortExplanation: 有 can indicate that a quality reaches a certain degree.
zh_you_degree_extent.fullExplanation: In some patterns, 有 is used before adjectives or quantity expressions to indicate that something reaches a degree or has a certain extent. This use is common in spoken Chinese.

examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù méiyǒu nàme yuǎn.

zh_you_comparison.title: 有 introducing comparison
zh_you_comparison.shortExplanation: 有 can be used in comparison patterns, often with 没有 or 有...这么/那么....
zh_you_comparison.fullExplanation: Chinese sometimes uses 有 in comparison to show degree or to ask whether something reaches a certain level. This often appears with 这么, 那么, or in contrast with 没有.

examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu nǐ zhème gāo ma?
Zhè jiā diàn méiyǒu nà jiā guì.

examples.chinese: 这条裙子有那条好看吗？
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo qúnzi yǒu nà tiáo hǎokàn ma?

zh_shi_emphasis_shi_de.title: 是...的 for emphasis
zh_shi_emphasis_shi_de.shortExplanation: 是...的 highlights a specific detail of a past action or situation.
zh_shi_emphasis_shi_de.fullExplanation: Use 是...的 to emphasize time, place, manner, agent, or other details of an action that is already known or assumed to have happened. The focus is not on whether the action happened, but on one highlighted element.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì zài Shànghǎi rènshi tā de.

examples.chinese: 这本书是朋友送给我的。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū shì péngyou sòng gěi wǒ de.

zh_shi_overall_assessment.title: 是 for assessing an overall situation
zh_shi_overall_assessment.shortExplanation: 是 can present an overall judgment before adding a reservation, comment, or evaluation.
zh_shi_overall_assessment.fullExplanation: In spoken Chinese, 是 may be used to acknowledge or assess a situation before adding another point. This often appears in patterns such as 是...，不过... or 是...，就是....

examples.chinese: 这个办法是不错，就是有点慢。
examples.pinyin: Zhège bànfǎ shì búcuò, jiùshì yǒudiǎn màn.

examples.chinese: 他人是很好，不过不太会表达。
examples.pinyin: Tā rén shì hěn hǎo, búguò bú tài huì biǎodá.

examples.chinese: 这里是方便，就是房租太贵了。
examples.pinyin: Zhèlǐ shì fāngbiàn, jiùshì fángzū tài guì le.

zh_you_adjectival_formative.title: 有 as an adjectival formative
zh_you_adjectival_formative.shortExplanation: 有 can help form descriptive meanings such as 有意思 or 有名.
zh_you_adjectival_formative.fullExplanation: In many common expressions, 有 works as part of a descriptive or adjectival meaning. These forms often describe possession of a quality rather than physical possession.

examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū hěn yǒu yìsi.

examples.chinese: 他是一个很有经验的老师。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yí ge hěn yǒu jīngyàn de lǎoshī.
Zhè zuò chéngshì hěn yǒu huólì.

zh_you_development_change.title: 有 expressing development and change
zh_you_development_change.shortExplanation: 有 can indicate that a situation has changed or developed in some way.
zh_you_development_change.fullExplanation: In spoken Chinese, 有 can be used to indicate that some degree of development or change exists, often in contrast with an earlier state. It often appears in evaluative or comparative contexts.

examples.pinyin: Tā de Zhōngwén yǒu jìnbù le.

examples.chinese: 这几年这里有很大的变化。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jǐ nián zhèlǐ yǒu hěn dà de biànhuà.
Tā bǐ yǐqián yǒu zìxìn duō le.

zh_you_conditional_clause.title: 有 introducing a conditional clause
zh_you_conditional_clause.shortExplanation: 有些 spoken patterns use 有 to introduce a condition or relevant circumstance.
zh_you_conditional_clause.fullExplanation: In colloquial Chinese, 有 can appear in patterns that introduce a condition or relevant case, especially in speech influenced by regional usage. For standard teaching, this is less central than 如果 or 要是, but learners may hear it in real conversation.

examples.chinese: 有问题的话，你就给我打电话。
examples.pinyin: Yǒu wèntí de huà, nǐ jiù gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà.

examples.chinese: 有需要可以随时联系我。
examples.pinyin: Yǒu xūyào kěyǐ suíshí liánxì wǒ.

zh_probability_keneng_yexu.title: Probability with 可能, 也许, 大概
zh_probability_keneng_yexu.shortExplanation: These words express possibility and probability.
zh_probability_keneng_yexu.fullExplanation: Use 可能, 也许, and 大概 when you want to say that something is possible or probable but not certain. They are common in daily conversation and explanations.

zh_preference_would_rather.title: Indicating preference: 宁可 / 宁愿
zh_preference_would_rather.shortExplanation: 宁可 and 宁愿 express strong preference, often with sacrifice or contrast.
zh_preference_would_rather.fullExplanation: Use 宁可 or 宁愿 when someone prefers one option even if it is difficult or less convenient. They often appear in patterns like 宁可...也不....

examples.chinese: 我宁愿坐地铁，也不想开车。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ nìngyuàn zuò dìtiě, yě bù xiǎng kāichē.

examples.chinese: 他宁可早点出门，也不愿意迟到。
examples.pinyin: Tā nìngkě zǎodiǎn chūmén, yě bù yuànyì chídào.

examples.chinese: 我宁愿自己做，也不想麻烦别人。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ nìngyuàn zìjǐ zuò, yě bù xiǎng máfan biérén.

zh_purpose_in_order_to.title: Purpose: 为了, 以便, 好让, 免得
zh_purpose_in_order_to.shortExplanation: These forms explain purpose: in order to, so as to, and so as not to.
zh_purpose_in_order_to.fullExplanation: 为了 introduces purpose directly. 以便 and 好让 express 'so that'. 免得 introduces a purpose of avoiding an unwanted result, similar to 'so as not to' or 'lest'.

examples.chinese: 为了提高口语，我每天都练习。
examples.pinyin: Wèile tígāo kǒuyǔ, wǒ měitiān dōu liànxí.

examples.chinese: 我把资料发给你，以便你提前看看。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bǎ zīliào fā gěi nǐ, yǐbiàn nǐ tíqián kànkan.

examples.chinese: 我们早点出发，免得路上堵车。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zǎodiǎn chūfā, miǎnde lùshang dǔchē.

zh_no_matter_buguan.title: Defying setbacks: 不管...都...
zh_no_matter_buguan.fullExplanation: Use 不管 before the varying condition and 都 before the result to say that the result remains the same in all cases.

examples.chinese: 不管多忙，我都要运动一下。
examples.pinyin: Bùguǎn duō máng, wǒ dōu yào yùndòng yíxià.

examples.chinese: 不管你去不去，我都会去。
examples.pinyin: Bùguǎn nǐ qù bú qù, wǒ dōu huì qù.

examples.chinese: 不管天气怎么样，他们都出门跑步。
examples.pinyin: Bùguǎn tiānqì zěnmeyàng, tāmen dōu chūmén pǎobù.

zh_apart_from_except_besides.title: Apart from / besides: 除了...以外...
zh_apart_from_except_besides.shortExplanation: 除了...以外... means 'apart from' or 'besides'.
zh_apart_from_except_besides.fullExplanation: Use 除了...以外... to add extra information or to exclude something depending on the sentence. It is often followed by 还, 也, or 都.

examples.chinese: 除了中文以外，我还会一点儿韩语。
examples.pinyin: Chúle Zhōngwén yǐwài, wǒ hái huì yìdiǎnr Hányǔ.
Chúle tā, dàjiā dōu dào le.

examples.chinese: 这家店除了咖啡，也卖甜点。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā diàn chúle kāfēi, yě mài tiándiǎn.

zh_let_alone.title: Let alone: 别说 / 更别说
zh_let_alone.shortExplanation: 别说 and 更别说 mean 'let alone'.
zh_let_alone.fullExplanation: Use 别说 or 更别说 to say that if one thing is not true or not possible, an even stronger case is even less likely.

examples.chinese: 他连中文都不会说，更别说写汉字了。
examples.pinyin: Tā lián Zhōngwén dōu bú huì shuō, gèng bié shuō xiě Hànzì le.

examples.chinese: 我今天连饭都没吃，别说出去玩了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān lián fàn dōu méi chī, bié shuō chūqù wán le.

examples.chinese: 这么难的问题，小孩子别说回答，连听都听不懂。
examples.pinyin: Zhème nán de wèntí, xiǎoháizi bié shuō huídá, lián tīng dōu tīng bù dǒng.

zh_otherwise.title: Otherwise: 否则
zh_otherwise.fullExplanation: Use 否则 to introduce a negative result that will happen if the first instruction or condition is not followed.

examples.chinese: 你得快一点，否则赶不上火车了。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ děi kuài yìdiǎn, fǒuzé gǎn bù shàng huǒchē le.

examples.chinese: 先把材料准备好，否则明天来不及。
examples.pinyin: Xiān bǎ cáiliào zhǔnbèi hǎo, fǒuzé míngtiān láibují.

examples.chinese: 你最好现在就问清楚，否则以后更麻烦。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuìhǎo xiànzài jiù wèn qīngchu, fǒuzé yǐhòu gèng máfan.

zh_result_potential_complex.title: Potential complements with 到, 懂, 完, 住
zh_result_potential_complex.shortExplanation: Potential complements show whether a result can or cannot be achieved.
zh_result_potential_complex.fullExplanation: Use 得 or 不 between the verb and result complement to show possibility or impossibility. This pattern is especially common with complements such as 到, 懂, 完, and 住.

examples.chinese: 这么小的字，我看不清，也看不到。
examples.pinyin: Zhème xiǎo de zì, wǒ kàn bù qīng, yě kàn bú dào.
Tā shuō de tài kuài, wǒ tīng bù dǒng.

examples.chinese: 这么多菜，我们吃不完，也带不走。
examples.pinyin: Zhème duō cài, wǒmen chī bù wán, yě dài bù zǒu.
